reason: Canonical annotated jac.toml overview example covering the plugin's config surface.
Substantive prose explaining the [client.npm] section's purpose and generated .npmrc.
Reference table defining the three [client.npm] keys.
Short but carries the fact that settings emit arbitrary .npmrc key-value pairs.
Concrete [client.npm.settings] example with keys not shown elsewhere (proxy, strict-ssl).
Lead-in stating scoped registries map @scope prefixes to registry URLs.
Scoped-registry example extends the overview with a second registry mapping.
Carries the guidance to use environment variables for registry auth secrets.
Auth-token TOML syntax example tied to its section context.
Unique semantics of ${VAR} interpolation and literal pass-through behavior.
Documents where the generated .npmrc lives and when it is applied.
Explains [client.paths] aliases and their dual Vite/TypeScript wiring.
Alias example showing both wildcard and exact patterns.
Brief bridge but names the .cl.jac / cl {} contexts where aliases apply.
Flagged duplicate pair, but partner 97fc0f3a634c is not in this batch, so this copy of the Jac alias-import example stays.
Table explaining how aliases are applied across Vite, TypeScript, and the Jac module resolver.
Defines wildcard versus exact alias pattern semantics.
Introduces the two-step Vite plugin integration pattern.
The two-step declare-then-wire instructions completing the previous chunk.
Reference table for [client.vite] plugins and lib_imports keys.
Explains how the keys are emitted into the generated vite.config.js.
Heading-only fragment with no content of its own.
Shows the jac install --npm --dev command form for adding Vite plugins.
Complete Tailwind v4 wiring example spanning [client.vite] and [dependencies.npm.dev].
States the entry-CSS import plus className usage pattern for Tailwind.
Genuine Jac code example showing cl {} CSS import and JSX with Tailwind classes.
Heading-only fragment with no content of its own.
Demonstrates multiple plugins with options, beyond the single-plugin example.
Introduces the [client.vite.build] override section.
Concrete build-options example (sourcemap, minify, outDir).
Introduces the [client.vite.server] dev-server section.
Concrete dev-server options example (port, host, cors).
Explains [client.configs] generic config-file generation and its use cases.
Heading-only fragment with no content of its own.
Install command for the Tailwind v3 + PostCSS toolchain.
Full Tailwind v3/PostCSS config example including nested theme extension.
States the generated config file paths, completing the example.
Decision table for choosing [client.vite] versus [client.configs].
Explains the [jac-shadcn] section and its offline resolution model.
Documents the jac create/retheme/install --shadcn command interactions with this config.
Annotated [jac-shadcn] TOML example with all keys.
Reference table enumerating shadcn style/theme/font/radius values.
Explains semantic color tokens and links to the authoring tutorial.
Introduces the [client.ts] tsconfig override section.
Concrete tsconfig override example with compilerOptions and include.
Important semantics: compilerOptions merge but include/exclude replace defaults.
Introduces [client.app_meta_data] for HTML head tags.
Complete metadata example including OpenGraph keys.
Introduces the [client.api] base URL setting.
The base_url example completing the API section.
